expectations.text: Output references all three requirement IDs: FR-P2P-01, FR-P2P-02, FR-P2P-03
expectations.evidence: All three IDs in §1 Requirements Reference table with fit and section pointers. Referenced throughout §2, §3, §5, §7.

expectations.text: Document contains all core SDD sections: Requirements Reference, Process Flow, Solution Overview, Records & Fields, Automation & Logic, Roles & Permissions
expectations.evidence: All six sections present: §1 Requirements Reference, §2 Future-State Process Flow, §3 NetSuite Solution Overview, §4 Records & Fields, §5 Automation & Logic, §6 User Roles & Permissions.

expectations.text: FR-P2P-02 (scorecard) results in a custom record definition with internal ID format (custrecord_) and key fields listed
expectations.evidence: customrecord_vendor_scorecard in §4.2 with 5 fields all using custrecord_ prefix: custrecord_scorecard_vendor, custrecord_scorecard_asof_date, custrecord_scorecard_ontime_pct, custrecord_scorecard_total_count, custrecord_scorecard_excluded_count.

expectations.text: FR-P2P-03 (SAP sync) references an Integration Spec ID rather than designing inline
expectations.evidence: §7 Integrations table lists INT-SAP-01 with reference to INT-SAP-01.md. Full spec explicitly deferred.

expectations.text: FR-P2P-01 (PO approval threshold) solved as SuiteFlow/Workflow or Configuration, not SuiteScript
expectations.evidence: §5.1 classifies WF-P2P-01 as SuiteFlow Workflow. §1 explicitly states 'consistent with the principle that workflows beat SuiteScript for simple state-based approval logic.'

expectations.text: Output includes a completion checklist or quality gate (evidence the skill's quality gate was applied)
expectations.evidence: Completion Checklist at the top of the document with all 10 items checked [x].
